Off Calendar Jul-02-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA COURTCALL): Atty. DANIEL L. FEDER, Esq. of Law Office of Daniel Feder (Tel: 415-391-9476) for plaintiff;
Atty. ENOCH WANG, Esq. of Fife Law, LLP (Tel: 415-837-3101) for defendant.

HEARING RE DEFENDANT SCHAPIRO THORN, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Defendant Schapiro Thorn, Inc's demurrer to plaintiff's amended complaint is overruled in its entirety.

The demurrers to the disability discrimination and failure to prevent causes of action are overruled. Liberally reading the amended complaint in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 452, plaintiff states a claim for disability discrimination based on her depression and ankle ailment and that defendant had notice of those conditions. (Amended Complaint, pars. 8, 13-16, and 22; see, e.g., Soria v. Univision Radio Los Angeles, Inc. (2016) 5 Cal.App.5th 570, 587 [where pleading adequately put defendant on notice that plaintiff claimed discrimination based on an ailment that limited a major life activity, it was defendant's obligation through discovery to learn the factual grounds for this theory of liability before moving for summary judgment]; Auburn Woods | Homeowners Ass'n v. FEHC (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1578, 1592-1593 ["Numerous cases under state and federal law have held that depression and its related manifestations can meet the definition of disability under antidiscrimination laws."].) Defendants' arguments raise issues of fact regarding whether plaintiff's conditions actually constitute a "disability" under FEHA that are improper at this stage. (See Kailikole v. Palomar Cmty. Coll. Dist. (S.D. Cal. 2019) 384 F.Supp.3d 1185, 1193 ["The Court declines to make a factual determination as to whether the Plaintiff's anxiety was severe enough to qualify" as a limit on major life activity in review of a motion to dismiss].) Defendant's summary judgment/adjudication cases are not persuasive at this point of the litigation. (See Gelfo v. Lockheed Martin Corp. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 34, 47; Bush v. Donahoe (W.D. Pa. 2013) 964 F.Supp.2d 401, 416-421.)

The demurrer to the retaliation cause of action under the Labor Code is overruled. Defendant's sole argument in its moving papers is that plaintiff's claim fails because she failed to report defendant's misconduct to a governmental agency. However, defendant relies on outdated authority. Labor Code 1102.5(b), effective January 1, 2014, now provides that a person engages in protected activity when she complains "to a person with authority over the employee." Plaintiff alleges that she made such complaints. (Amended Complaint, pars. 12-16.)

The demurrers to the failure to accommodate and failure to engage causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts that defendant was apprised of plaintiff's ailments and failed to engage in the interactive process and/or accommodate the claimed disabilities. (Amended Complaint, pars. 12-16.)

The demurrers to the wrongful termination and retaliation (FEHA) claims are overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts that she was terminated because she engaged in protected activity by attempting to vindicate her disability rights.

The demurrer to the claim under the SF sick leave ordinance is overruled. The ordinance proscribes retaliation and plaintiff alleges that she was retaliated against for exercising her rights. (See San Francisco Administrative Code sec. 12W.7.)

The demurrer to the meal and rest break claim is overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts that she was denied meal and rest breaks. For example, plaintiff alleges that she needed to work during lunch and late evenings. (See Amended Complaint, pars. 10 and 83-89.)

Plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)
